vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1393: Flywheel: change orchestrator from auto-drive to suggest-only + fix runtime so it actually executes ticks

plan.title: Flywheel: change orchestrator from auto-drive to suggest-only + fix runtime so it actually executes ticks
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The Fix-All Flywheel orchestrator currently auto-drives the pipeline (tick step 4: 'Drive the pipeline') and frequently never reaches its emit-status step, leaving the dashboard Flywheel pane blank during active runs (PAN-1386). The operator wants the orchestrator to only SUGGEST next moves — never auto-pull issues into the pipeline — and the runtime needs to actually emit status reliably each tick so the suggestions surface stays live.
narratives.Proposal: Two coordinated changes: (1) extend the FlywheelStatus contract with a `suggestions: FlywheelSuggestion[]` field, then rewrite both the role prompt (`roles/flywheel.md`) and the default brief (`docs/flywheel-brief.md`) so each tick produces ranked suggestions instead of pipeline actions — substrate-bug FILING remains allowed (it is recordkeeping), substrate-bug FIXING moves into the suggestion list, and lifecycle commands (`pan start`, `pan plan`, `pan tell`, `pan approve`, `pan sync-main`, `pan resume`) are explicitly forbidden. (2) Render the suggestion list as the primary section of the dashboard Flywheel pane, add a freshness indicator so a stalled orchestrator is visibly stalled instead of silently invisible, and verify end-to-end with a Playwright UAT that posts a status snapshot and asserts the suggestions section is visible.

autoDecisions.summary: Suggestion shape: { action: enum, issueId?: string, rationale: string, priority: enum }, with action ∈ {start,resume,plan,review,merge,unblock,park,investigate,wait} and priority ∈ {urgent,high,medium,low}.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body specifies `{action, issueId?, rationale, priority}` but leaves the value sets open. Literal unions keep the schema tight (so the orchestrator can't emit free-form garbage) while covering the verbs the brief actually uses (start/resume/review/merge/unblock/park) plus 'investigate' (for substrate bugs) and 'wait' (for items that should not be acted on yet). Priority labels mirror the brief's existing P0/P1/P2/P3 ladder in human-readable form.

autoDecisions.summary: `suggestions` field is schema-optional with a default of `[]` (TypeScript type stays required) so historical `latest.json` files for old runs still decode.
autoDecisions.rationale: Adding a required field would break `readLatestFlywheelStatus` for every prior run on disk, breaking `listFlywheelRuns` and the run-detail surface. `Schema.optionalWith(..., { default: () => [] })` keeps backward compat while the TS type stays required so writers (the initial-status seed, work agents) cannot forget to include it.

autoDecisions.summary: Runtime fix is prompt restructure + dashboard freshness indicator. No server-side auto-nag or auto-emit.
autoDecisions.rationale: PAN-1386's root cause is that emit-status was tick step 5 — after 'drive the pipeline', which the orchestrator interpreted as license to disappear into substrate-fix work. In suggest-only mode the tick has nothing to do EXCEPT emit suggestions, so emit becomes the natural deliverable. Backstop: dashboard surfaces 'Last tick: 3m ago — stalled' so silent failure is impossible to miss. Server-side nag (pan tell from a timer) risks loops and was deferred per the issue's scope.

autoDecisions.summary: Substrate-bug FIXING is removed from the orchestrator's allowed actions. Filing remains allowed; the resulting bug appears as an investigate/start suggestion in the next tick.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue body lists exactly four allowed actions: inventory, diagnose, emit suggestions, file substrate bugs. Fixing code requires editing/building/committing — these are pipeline actions per the spirit of suggest-only. Removing fix-in-orchestrator makes the role's responsibility crisp and unifies all 'work' under the normal pipeline.

autoDecisions.summary: Suggestions render as the FIRST section of FlywheelStatusDetails, above the headline metrics strip.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body: 'the new suggestions[] list as the primary surface'. Putting it above MetricStrip makes 'what should the operator do next' the first thing on screen; metrics/pipeline/agents fall below as context.

autoDecisions.summary: Click-to-apply on suggestions is OUT of scope. Suggestions render as read-only rows with the issueId linked to the existing kanban/inspector navigation.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body: 'click-to-apply is OUT of scope for this issue (will be filed separately if needed).' Linking the issueId is free (already-supported navigation) and turns a suggestion into an actionable jump without committing to action wiring.

autoDecisions.summary: roles/flywheel.md and docs/flywheel-brief.md are updated in two separate beads with a blocking edge (brief depends on role).
autoDecisions.rationale: They describe the same loop in two different surfaces; keeping them in separate, independently reviewable beads makes the diff easy to verify in each file. The blocking edge forces brief-rewrite to land after role-rewrite, eliminating divergence windows.

autoDecisions.summary: Freshness indicator threshold: lastTickAt > 90s ago → 'stalled' chip (warning tone); > 30s ago → 'last tick Xs ago' (info tone); ≤ 30s → 'live'.
autoDecisions.rationale: Typical tick cadence in suggest-only mode should be well under 30s (no work to do, just inventory+emit). 90s allows for a slow tick under heavy `gh issue` queries without flapping; 'stalled' is a clear visual cue the operator should investigate.

autoDecisions.summary: Playwright UAT injects a stub FlywheelStatus via POST /api/flywheel/status with the internal token (matching the existing flywheel-page-status.spec.ts pattern) rather than spawning a real orchestrator.
autoDecisions.rationale: The existing UAT already uses this exact pattern with a MockWebSocket and a stub payload. Spawning a real orchestrator in a test would (a) consume agent budget, (b) be flaky under model load, and (c) test the wrong layer — the issue's acceptance criterion is 'pane shows suggestions list with at least one entry,' which is a rendering assertion.

items.title: Add FlywheelSuggestion schema and suggestions field to FlywheelStatus contract

narrative.Action: In packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts, add FlywheelSuggestionAction and FlywheelSuggestionPriority literal unions, a FlywheelSuggestion interface + Schema struct ({action, issueId?, rationale, priority}), and add `suggestions: ReadonlyArray<FlywheelSuggestion>` to both the FlywheelStatus interface (required) and the Schema (optionalWith default `[]` for backward compatibility with historical latest.json). Update packages/contracts/src/flywheel.test.ts to (a) keep the existing validPayload passing by adding a `suggestions: [...]` entry, (b) cover absent-suggestions backward compat decode, (c) cover invalid action/priority rejection.

subItems.title: FlywheelSuggestion exported as both TS type and Effect Schema, with action ∈ {start,resume,plan,review,merge,unblock,park,investigate,wait} and priority ∈ {urgent,high,medium,low}
FlywheelStatus TypeScript interface requires `suggestions: ReadonlyArray<FlywheelSuggestion>`
FlywheelStatus Schema decodes payloads WITHOUT a suggestions field (defaults to `[]`) — verifies historical latest.json files do not break
FlywheelStatus Schema rejects suggestions with unknown action or priority values
packages/contracts/src/flywheel.test.ts covers: valid suggestions array, empty array, missing field (default), invalid action, invalid priority — all green under `bun --filter @panctl/contracts test`
`bun --filter @panctl/contracts run build` succeeds and exports FlywheelSuggestion from packages/contracts/src/index.ts

items.title: Seed initial FlywheelStatus snapshot with empty suggestions[] in both spawn paths

narrative.Action: Add `suggestions: []` to the object returned by createInitialFlywheelStatus in BOTH src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts and src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-actions.ts. Update any existing unit tests in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts and src/cli/commands/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts that build a FlywheelStatus fixture so they include the new field (the TypeScript type requires it).

subItems.title: createInitialFlywheelStatus in src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts returns an object with `suggestions: []`
createInitialFlywheelStatus in src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-actions.ts returns an object with `suggestions: []`
`npm run typecheck` passes — no `Property 'suggestions' is missing` errors in any flywheel test or service file
All existing flywheel unit/route tests still pass after the fixture update

items.title: Rewrite roles/flywheel.md tick loop to suggest-only (no pipeline driving, no substrate fixing)

narrative.Action: Rewrite roles/flywheel.md so the tick loop becomes: 1. Inventory; 2. Diagnose; 3. Emit suggestions (the deliverable of every tick — a ranked list with action/issueId?/rationale/priority); 4. File substrate bugs as records when broken Panopticon behavior is discovered; 5. Respect pauses. Remove the 'Fix substrate first' step and replace it with 'Suggest substrate fixes — a substrate bug becomes an investigate/start suggestion in the suggestions list. The orchestrator never edits substrate code itself.' Expand the 'Never' list to explicitly forbid: pan start, pan plan, pan tell, pan approve, pan sync-main, pan resume, pan wake, pan kill, pan wipe, pan close, plus any direct git/PR mutation beyond `gh issue view` / `gh issue create` for substrate bug filing. Keep `pan flywheel emit-status` and `pan flywheel report` allowed. Make explicit that emit-status is the OUTPUT of every tick, not an afterthought.

subItems.title: Tick loop in roles/flywheel.md no longer contains a 'Drive the pipeline' step
Tick loop has an explicit 'Emit suggestions' step that requires producing a ranked suggestions[] every tick and calling `pan flywheel emit-status`

Explicit 'Never' / do-not list includes (verbatim or equivalent): pan start, pan plan, pan tell, pan approve, pan sync-main, pan resume, pan wake, pan kill, pan wipe, pan close, direct edits to feature branches, direct PR merges
Substrate handling: filing bugs via `gh issue create` is explicitly allowed; editing substrate code is explicitly forbidden; substrate bugs surface as investigate/start suggestions
Document references the new FlywheelSuggestion shape (action/issueId?/rationale/priority) so the orchestrator emits valid payloads
Existing allowlist (author=eltmon or panopticon-agent[bot]) and label exclusions (needs-design, needs-discussion) are preserved as filters for what makes it into suggestions

items.title: Rewrite docs/flywheel-brief.md tick loop to mirror the suggest-only role

narrative.Action: Rewrite docs/flywheel-brief.md so the tick loop, substrate-fix rule, and human-input invariant match the suggest-only role. Replace step 3 ('Act. Take the smallest Panopticon-native action…') with 'Emit ranked suggestions — produce a `suggestions[]` array in the FlywheelStatus snapshot, with the next-best moves for the operator.' Replace the substrate-fix-rule body so it describes filing-and-suggesting instead of fixing. Keep status/state separation and end-of-run report semantics unchanged. This bead is blocked by role-prompt-rewrite so the brief lands second and mirrors the role's final text.

subItems.title: docs/flywheel-brief.md step 3 says 'Emit ranked suggestions' (or equivalent) and no longer says 'Take the smallest Panopticon-native action'
Substrate-fix rule body in the brief describes file-and-suggest, NOT fix-in-orchestrator
Brief's do-not list matches the role's do-not list (no pipeline-driving verbs, no direct branch/PR mutation)
Status-vs-State section and end-of-run report section unchanged in substance
Author allowlist and parked-label exclusion sections preserved

items.title: Render Suggestions as the primary section in FlywheelStatusDetails

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx, add a new `<Section title="Suggestions">` rendered BEFORE the existing MetricStrip. Sort suggestions by priority (urgent → high → medium → low). Each row shows: priority badge (color-coded), action verb, issueId (when present) as a click-through to the existing kanban/inspector navigation, and rationale text. When suggestions[] is empty, render a placeholder: 'No suggestions yet — orchestrator will emit on next tick.' Add component unit tests in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/__tests__/FlywheelStatusDetails.test.tsx covering: rendering with multiple suggestions, priority sort order, empty state, click-through for suggestions with an issueId, click-through omission for system-level suggestions without an issueId.

subItems.title: Suggestions section appears ABOVE the headline MetricStrip in FlywheelStatusDetails
Suggestions are sorted urgent → high → medium → low; ties hold input order (stable sort)
Each row displays priority badge, action, issueId (when present), and rationale
issueId is rendered as a button/link that invokes onNavigateAgent (or equivalent navigation prop) when clicked
Empty state placeholder visible when suggestions[] is empty (must not collapse the whole section)
Component unit tests cover render, sort, empty state, click-through with issueId, no click-through without issueId — all green under `npm run test --workspace @panctl/frontend`

items.title: Show last-tick freshness chip on the Flywheel pane header

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx (header) and/or FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx, replace the existing static `Last tick` time cell with a freshness chip computed from `Date.now() - new Date(status.lastTickAt).getTime()`: ≤30s → green 'live'; 30–90s → amber 'last tick Xs ago'; >90s → red 'stalled — last tick Xm ago'. Update the displayed elapsed time on a 5s interval so the chip transitions in real time. Add a unit test in src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/__tests__/FlywheelPage.test.tsx using vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.setSystemTime() to assert all three states render at the correct thresholds (per the workspace fake-timers rule).

subItems.title: Header shows a green 'live' indicator when lastTickAt is ≤30s old
Header shows an amber 'last tick Xs ago' indicator when lastTickAt is 30–90s old
Header shows a red 'stalled — last tick Xm ago' indicator when lastTickAt is >90s old
Display refreshes at least every 5s while the page is mounted (so the chip transitions without waiting for a new snapshot)
Unit test uses vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.setSystemTime() to verify all three thresholds — no real wall-clock waits

items.title: Include Suggestions in formatFlywheelStateReport markdown so they archive into the per-run report

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts, extend formatFlywheelStateReport to render a '## Suggestions' section listing each suggestion (priority, action, issueId, rationale) before or after 'Active Pipeline'. Add a unit test in src/cli/commands/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts asserting both populated and empty-list rendering, including escape behavior for `|` and newlines in rationale text (mirrors existing tableCell escapes).

subItems.title: formatFlywheelStateReport renders a '## Suggestions' section after the Headline/Run-counters block and before 'Active Pipeline'
Empty suggestions[] renders 'No suggestions emitted this run.' instead of an empty table row
Rationale text containing `|` or newlines is escaped via tableCell (or equivalent) so markdown tables render correctly
Unit tests cover populated and empty rendering; existing formatFlywheelStateReport tests still pass

items.title: Playwright UAT: post a stubbed FlywheelStatus with suggestions and assert the pane renders the list

narrative.Action: Extend src/dashboard/frontend/tests/flywheel-page-status.spec.ts (or add a sibling spec) to: (1) add `suggestions: [{action:'start', issueId:'PAN-1234', rationale:'Oldest P1 substrate bug', priority:'urgent'}, …]` to the stub payload; (2) wait for the Flywheel page to mount; (3) assert the Suggestions section is visible, contains at least one entry, and the priority badge / action / issueId / rationale text are rendered; (4) verify the section is ABOVE the headline metrics in DOM order; (5) verify the freshness chip is in 'live' state at t=0. Use Playwright's `test.use({ contextOptions: { ... } })` or a fresh `browser.newContext()` per spec so the test runs in an isolated browser profile (per workspace Playwright Isolation rule). Existing 'renders a live FlywheelStatus payload without browser console errors' assertion is preserved.

subItems.title: Stub payload includes a populated `suggestions: [...]` with at least one urgent and one medium entry
Test asserts the Suggestions section is visible and contains a row matching each stubbed suggestion's rationale text
Test asserts DOM order: Suggestions section appears before the MetricStrip
Test asserts the freshness chip starts in 'live' state with the stub payload's lastTickAt set to a fresh timestamp
Test uses an isolated browser context (no shared profile) per the workspace Playwright Isolation rule
No-console-errors assertion from the existing test is preserved and still passes
Pre-existing `pan flywheel pause/resume/abort/report/emit-status` CLI tests in src/cli/commands/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts still pass — verifies no regression in semantics
